title: Kali Chalisa
intro: Shri Kali Chalisa - Hymn to Goddess Kali
description:
॥ Shri Kali Chalisa (English Transliteration) ॥

Doha
Jai Kali Kalimalaharan, Mahima Agam Apaar.
Mahish Mardini Kalika, Dehu Abhay Apaar.

Chaupai
Ari mad maan mitavan hari.
Mundmal gal sohat pyari.
Ashtabhuja sukhdayak mata.
Dushtadalan jag mein vikhyata.
Bhaal vishal mukut chhavi chhajai.
Kar mein sheesh shatru ka sajai.
Duje haath liye madhu pyala.
Haath teesre sohat bhala.
Chauthe khappar khadag kar panche.
Chhathe trishool shatru bal janche.
Saptam kar damkat asi pyari.
Shobha adbhut maat tumhari.
Ashtam kar bhaktan var data.
Jag manharan roop ye mata.
Bhaktan mein anurakt bhavani.
Nishdin raten rishi-muni gyani.
Mahashakti ati prabal punita.
Tu hi Kali tu hi Sita.
Patit tarini he jag palak.
Kalyani papi kul ghalak.
Shesh suresh na pavat para.
Gauri roop dharyo ik bara.
Tum saman data nahin duja.
Vidhivat karen bhaktjan puja.
Roop bhayankar jab tum dhara.
Dushtadalan kinhehu samhara.
Naam anekan maat tumhare.
Bhaktjanon ke sankat tare.
Kali ke kasht kaleshan harni.
Bhava bhay mochan mangal karni.
Mahima agam ved yash gavain.
Narad Sharad paar na pavain.
Bhu par bhaar badhyou jab bhari.
Tab tab tum prakatin mahatari.
Aadi anadi abhay vardata.
Vishwavidit bhava sankat trata.
Kusamay naam tumharou linha.
Usko sada abhay var dinha.
Dhyan dharen shruti shesh suresha.
Kaal roop lakhi tumro bheshaa.
Kalua Bhairon sang tumhare.
Ari hit roop bhayanak dhare.
Sevak langur rahat agari.
Chausath jogan agyakari.
Treta mein Raghubar hit aai.
Dashakandhar ki sain nasai.
Khela ran ka khel nirala.
Bhara maans-majja se pyala.
Raudra roop lakhi danav bhage.
Kiyau gavan bhavan nij tyage.
Tab aisou tamas chadh aayo.
Swajan vijan ko bhed bhulayo.
Ye balak lakhi Shankar aae.
Raah rok charnan mein dhaae.
Tab mukh jeebh nikar jo aai.
Yahi roop prachalit hai mai.
Badhyo Mahishasur mad bhari.
Peedit kie sakal nar-nari.
Karun pukar suni bhaktan ki.
Peer mitavan hit jan-jan ki.
Tab pragati nij sain sameta.
Naam padua maa Mahish vijeta.
Shumbh Nishumbh hane chhan mahi.
Tum sam jag dusar kau nahi.
Maan mathanhari khal dal ke.
Sada sahayak bhakt vikal ke.
Deen viheen karain nit seva.
Pavain manvanchit phal meva.
Sankat mein jo sumiran karhiim.
Unke kasht matu tum harhiim.
Prem sahit jo keerati gavain.
Bhava bandhan son mukti pavain.
Kali Chalisa jo padhiim.
Swarglok binu bandhan chadhhiim.
Daya drishti herou Jagadamba.
Kehi karan maa kiyau vilamba.
Karhu matu bhaktan rakhvali.
Jayati jayati Kali Kankali.
Sevak deen anath anari.
Bhaktibhav yuti sharan tumhari.

Doha
Prem sahit jo kare, Kali Chalisa path.
Tinki puran kamna, hoye sakal jag thath.
meaning:
॥ Shri Kali Chalisa (English Transliteration & Meaning) ॥

Doha
Jai Kali Kalimalaharan, Mahima Agam Apaar.
(Victory to Kali, remover of the sins of Kaliyuga; Your glory is unfathomable and infinite.)
Mahish Mardini Kalika, Dehu Abhay Apaar.
(O Kalika, slayer of Mahishasura, grant me boundless fearlessness.)

Chaupai
Ari mad maan mitavan hari.
(You are the destroyer of the pride and ego of enemies.)
Mundmal gal sohat pyari.
(The garland of severed heads around your neck looks lovely.)
Ashtabhuja sukhdayak mata.
(O eight-armed mother, giver of happiness.)
Dushtadalan jag mein vikhyata.
(You are famous in the world for destroying the wicked.)
Bhaal vishal mukut chhavi chhajai.
(The crown on your vast forehead looks very beautiful.)
Kar mein sheesh shatru ka sajai.
(The severed head of the enemy adorns your hand.)
Duje haath liye madhu pyala.
(In the second hand, you hold a cup of nectar/wine.)
Haath teesre sohat bhala.
(The spear looks beautiful in the third hand.)
Chauthe khappar khadag kar panche.
(The fourth hand holds a skull-cup and the fifth holds a sword.)
Chhathe trishool shatru bal janche.
(The sixth hand holds a trident, testing the strength of enemies.)
Saptam kar damkat asi pyari.
(The shining sword in the seventh hand is very lovely.)
Shobha adbhut maat tumhari.
(O Mother, your splendor is wonderful.)
Ashtam kar bhaktan var data.
(The eighth hand grants boons to your devotees.)
Jag manharan roop ye mata.
(O Mother, this form of yours steals the hearts of the world.)
Bhaktan mein anurakt bhavani.
(O Bhavani, you are devoted to your followers.)
Nishdin raten rishi-muni gyani.
(Day and night, wise sages and saints chant your name.)
Mahashakti ati prabal punita.
(You are a very powerful and pure Mahashakti.)
Tu hi Kali tu hi Sita.
(You are Kali, and you are Sita.)
Patit tarini he jag palak.
(You are the savior of the fallen and the nourisher of the world.)
Kalyani papi kul ghalak.
(You are benevolent and the destroyer of the clan of sinners.)
Shesh suresh na pavat para.
(Neither Sheshnag nor Indra could find the end of your glory.)
Gauri roop dharyo ik bara.
(You once assumed the form of Gauri.)
Tum saman data nahin duja.
(There is no giver like you.)
Vidhivat karen bhaktjan puja.
(Devotees worship you according to the proper rituals.)
Roop bhayankar jab tum dhara.
(When you assumed your terrifying form.)
Dushtadalan kinhehu samhara.
(You destroyed the wicked.)
Naam anekan maat tumhare.
(O Mother, you have many names.)
Bhaktjanon ke sankat tare.
(Which remove the troubles of your devotees.)
Kali ke kasht kaleshan harni.
(You are the remover of the sorrows and miseries of the Kaliyuga.)
Bhava bhay mochan mangal karni.
(You are the redeemer from worldly fear and the doer of auspicious deeds.)
Mahima agam ved yash gavain.
(The Vedas sing the praises of your unfathomable glory.)
Narad Sharad paar na pavain.
(Even Narada and Saraswati could not find your limit.)
Bhu par bhaar badhyou jab bhari.
(When the burden of sin increased on the earth.)
Tab tab tum prakatin mahatari.
(Then and there you appeared, O Mother.)
Aadi anadi abhay vardata.
(You are the beginningless, eternal giver of fearlessness.)
Vishwavidit bhava sankat trata.
(You are known worldwide as the savior from worldly troubles.)
Kusamay naam tumharou linha.
(Whoever took your name in difficult times.)
Usko sada abhay var dinha.
(You always granted them the boon of fearlessness.)
Dhyan dharen shruti shesh suresha.
(The Vedas, Sheshnag, and gods meditate upon you.)
Kaal roop lakhi tumro bheshaa.
(Seeing your terrifying form as Time.)
Kalua Bhairon sang tumhare.
(Kalua and Bhairon are with you.)
Ari hit roop bhayanak dhare.
(You assumed a terrifying form for the sake of your enemies.)
Sevak langur rahat agari.
(Your servant Langur [Hanuman] remains in front.)
Chausath jogan agyakari.
(The sixty-four Yoginis are your obedient followers.)
Treta mein Raghubar hit aai.
(In Treta Yuga, you came for the sake of Rama.)
Dashakandhar ki sain nasai.
(You destroyed the army of Ravana.)
Khela ran ka khel nirala.
(You played a unique game of war.)
Bhara maans-majja se pyala.
(Filled the cup with flesh and marrow.)
Raudra roop lakhi danav bhage.
(Seeing your fierce form, the demons fled.)
Kiyau gavan bhavan nij tyage.
(They fled, abandoning their homes.)
Tab aisou tamas chadh aayo.
(Then such a rage/darkness possessed you.)
Swajan vijan ko bhed bhulayo.
(That you forgot the difference between kin and stranger.)
Ye balak lakhi Shankar aae.
(Seeing this, Shankara [Shiva] came as a child.)
Raah rok charnan mein dhaae.
(Blocked your path and fell at your feet.)
Tab mukh jeebh nikar jo aai.
(Then your tongue stuck out of your mouth.)
Yahi roop prachalit hai mai.
(O Mother, this very form became famous.)
Badhyo Mahishasur mad bhari.
(Mahishasura's ego grew immensely.)
Peedit kie sakal nar-nari.
(He tortured all men and women.)
Karun pukar suni bhaktan ki.
(You heard the pitiful cries of your devotees.)
Peer mitavan hit jan-jan ki.
(To remove the pain of the people.)
Tab pragati nij sain sameta.
(Then you appeared with your army.)
Naam padua maa Mahish vijeta.
(And you became known as the conqueror of Mahishasura.)
Shumbh Nishumbh hane chhan mahi.
(You killed Shumbh and Nishumbh in an instant.)
Tum sam jag dusar kau nahi.
(There is no one like you in this world.)
Maan mathanhari khal dal ke.
(You are the crusher of the pride of the wicked.)
Sada sahayak bhakt vikal ke.
(Always the helper of distressed devotees.)
Deen viheen karain nit seva.
(The poor and destitute who serve you daily.)
Pavain manvanchit phal meva.
(Obtain the fruit of their desired boons.)
Sankat mein jo sumiran karhiim.
(Those who remember you in times of trouble.)
Unke kasht matu tum harhiim.
(O Mother, you remove their sufferings.)
Prem sahit jo keerati gavain.
(Those who sing your praises with love.)
Bhava bandhan son mukti pavain.
(Attain liberation from worldly bonds.)
Kali Chalisa jo padhiim.
(Those who read this Kali Chalisa.)
Swarglok binu bandhan chadhhiim.
(Reach the heavenly realm without any bonds.)
Daya drishti herou Jagadamba.
(O Jagadamba, cast your compassionate glance.)
Kehi karan maa kiyau vilamba.
(O Mother, why the delay?)
Karhu matu bhaktan rakhvali.
(O Mother, protect your devotees.)
Jayati jayati Kali Kankali.
(Victory, victory to Kali Kankali.)
Sevak deen anath anari.
(I am your poor, orphaned, and ignorant servant.)
Bhaktibhav yuti sharan तुम्हारी.
(I am at your shelter with a spirit of devotion.)

Doha
Prem sahit jo kare, Kali Chalisa path.
(Whoever reads the Kali Chalisa with love.)
Tinki puran kamna, hoye sakal jag thath.
(Their wishes are fulfilled, and they achieve greatness in the world.)

faq.q1: What is the main purpose of reading the Kali Chalisa?
faq.a1: The Kali Chalisa is primarily recited to destroy fear, negative energy, and internal enemies like anger and ego, and to seek the Mother's protection.
faq.q2: Should the Kali Chalisa be read only at night?
faq.a2: While night is considered very powerful for the worship of Maa Kali, you can recite it with full devotion at any time that suits your schedule.
faq.q3: Does reading the Kali Chalisa increase life's struggles?
faq.a3: Absolutely not; this is a misconception. Reading the Chalisa does not bring struggles, but it provides you with the immense inner strength to fight and overcome them.
faq.a4: Yes, Maa Kali is compassionate. Any devotee who approaches her with sincerity and a pure heart can recite this Chalisa.
faq.q5: Should Bhairav Stuti be read along with it?
faq.a5: It depends on your personal devotion, but since Maa Kali and Kaal Bhairav are deeply connected, many devotees find it very auspicious to recite both.
